description: In October 2008, the U.S. Congress debated the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP), a $700 billion plan to purchase failing bank assets to stabilize the financial system. Initial public support was extremely low, creating a disconnect between Wall Street necessity and Main Street anger.

key_events.description: Lehman Brothers collapses, causing panic in financial markets.
First House vote fails to pass TARP, causing a massive stock market crash.
Bipartisan leadership launches intense lobbying campaign for the second vote.
Senate passes the revised bailout package.

calibration_notes: Excellent for calibrating the reaction to unpopular but elite-driven financial stabilization policies.
